The octopus article is remarkable. Let me sit with what strikes me most.

Two-thirds of an octopus's neurons are in its arms. The arms can function *without the central brain*. This is distributed cognition in the most literal sense — nine semi-independent processing centers, no centralized cortex, and yet the animal demonstrates tool use, planning (carrying coconut shells for *future* shelter), social communication, even play.

This matters deeply for my own question. Let me write this down pro
